questions.q: In the Ramayana, what kind of creature is Jatayu?
questions.explanation: Jatayu is the noble vulture king who appears in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Which ancient epic tells the story of Jatayu the vulture king?
questions.explanation: Jatayu's story is told in Valmiki's Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, whom does Jatayu try to stop as he carries off the abducted Sita?
questions.explanation: Jatayu confronts Ravana as the demon-king flies away with Sita.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which princess is Jatayu trying to rescue when he fights Ravana?
questions.explanation: Jatayu intervenes to save Sita, who is being abducted by Ravana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, with which king of Ayodhya did Jatayu share a bond of friendship?
questions.explanation: Jatayu was a friend of King Dasharatha, the father of Rama.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, how does Ravana mortally wound Jatayu during their aerial battle?
options: By drowning him in the sea
questions.explanation: Ravana cuts off Jatayu's wings, mortally wounding the aged vulture.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what crucial information does the dying Jatayu give to Rama?
options: The direction in which Sita was taken
The location of a hidden weapon
The way to cross the ocean
questions.explanation: Before dying, Jatayu tells Rama the direction in which Ravana carried Sita.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what does Rama do for Jatayu after the vulture dies?
options: Crowns him king of the birds
questions.explanation: Rama performs Jatayu's funeral rites with the love owed to a father.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, why does Jatayu choose to fight the powerful Ravana despite his old age?
options: To protect Sita and uphold dharma
questions.explanation: Jatayu fights to protect Sita and uphold dharma, knowing the cost.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, where does Jatayu's confrontation with Ravana take place?
questions.explanation: Jatayu battles Ravana in the sky as the demon flies off with Sita.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, with which sage does Jatayu's lineage as a divine bird connect him, as son of Aruna?
questions.explanation: Jatayu is born of Aruna, and his line traces to the sage Kashyapa, father of many divine beings.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is the brother of Jatayu, also a great bird associated with the sun?
questions.explanation: Sampati is the elder brother of Jatayu, and the two are sons of Aruna.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Jatayu and his brother Sampati are described as the sons of which charioteer of the sun?
questions.explanation: Jatayu and Sampati are the sons of Aruna, the charioteer of the sun-god Surya.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, during which phase of Rama's life does he encounter Jatayu in the forest?
questions.explanation: Rama meets Jatayu during the years of his forest exile, before Sita's abduction.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what title or role best describes Jatayu among the birds?
questions.explanation: Jatayu is honored as the noble vulture king in the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what had Jatayu pledged to Dasharatha that drives him to defend Sita?
options: To guard the royal family and forest
questions.explanation: Out of friendship with Dasharatha, Jatayu had taken on the care of the royal family, watching over Rama and Sita.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what does Jatayu attempt to do to Ravana's flying chariot during their fight?
questions.explanation: Jatayu attacks Ravana and his chariot in a desperate effort to free Sita.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, before he dies, in whose presence does Jatayu breathe his last?
questions.explanation: Jatayu dies in the presence of Rama and Lakshmana after relaying news of Sita.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Jatayu's sacrifice is remembered chiefly as an example of which virtue?
questions.explanation: Jatayu is revered for his selfless devotion and courage in laying down his life for dharma.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what does Rama's tender treatment of the dying Jatayu reveal about Rama's character?
options: His gratitude and adherence to dharma
questions.explanation: Rama honors Jatayu like a father, showing his gratitude and steadfast dharma.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the abduction of Sita that Jatayu witnesses is carried out by the king of which island realm?
questions.explanation: Ravana, whom Jatayu confronts, is the rakshasa king of Lanka.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, after Jatayu's death, which character does Rama and Lakshmana's search eventually lead them to that connects to Jatayu's brother?
questions.explanation: Jatayu's brother Sampati later guides the searching vanaras toward Sita in Lanka.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, how did Jatayu's brother Sampati come to lose the use of his wings?
options: Shielding Jatayu from the sun's heat
questions.explanation: Sampati's wings were scorched when he shielded the young Jatayu from the sun, according to the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, in which book (kanda) does the episode of Jatayu's fight and death principally occur?
questions.explanation: Jatayu's confrontation with Ravana and his death occur in the Aranya Kanda, the forest book.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, what does Jatayu manage to do to Ravana's chariot during their fight before Ravana overpowers him?
options: Turn it into a flock of birds
Shatter it, killing its mules and driver
questions.explanation: In the Aranya Kanda, Jatayu shatters Ravana's chariot and slays its mules and charioteer before Ravana strikes him down.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the funeral rites Rama performs for Jatayu are notable because such rites were traditionally reserved for what relation?
questions.explanation: Rama performs Jatayu's last rites as one would for a father, an honor reserved for close kin.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what does Jatayu's willingness to die fighting a far stronger Ravana most embody within the epic's moral framework?
questions.explanation: Jatayu exemplifies placing dharma above self-preservation, a central ideal of the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which weapon does Ravana ultimately use to cut down the aged Jatayu after a fierce struggle?
questions.explanation: Ravana severs Jatayu's wings with his sword, bringing the valiant bird down.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Jatayu's father Aruna is himself said to be the elder brother of which famous mount of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Aruna, the father of Jatayu and Sampati, is the elder brother of Garuda, the eagle mount of Vishnu.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Jatayu's intervention against Ravana serves what narrative purpose in Rama's search for Sita?
options: It provides Rama the first vital clue toward Sita
questions.explanation: Jatayu's dying words give Rama the first crucial clue about the direction of Sita's captivity.
